region_of_origin: United States (Starbucks global brand; served in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for low-fat caramel macchiato. FOODON_03301737 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coffee and coffee substitutes as a category. No specific code exists for caramel macchiato with low-fat milk; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 represents coffee as a substance. No specific term for caramel macchiato beverage; closest_parent applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall (12 fl oz / 355 ml) serving of low-fat caramel macchiato, weighing approximately 366 g, as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Caramel Macchiato with Non-fat Milk — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks Caramel Macchiato made with non-fat/low-fat milk (FDC ID 2097024 and related entries). The 'less sweet' modifier reflects a reduced-syrup preparation; sugar_g has been proportionally adjusted downward by approximately 20% relative to the standard preparation to reflect the reduced-syrup variant. All per-100g values are derived from the standard tall (355 ml) serving data normalised to 100 g, using a density approximation of 1.03 g/ml for the milk-based beverage. Values are consistent with published Starbucks Singapore nutrition data for the caramel macchiato range.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — milk-based coffee beverages and sweetened dairy drinks
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a low-fat caramel macchiato less-sweet variant. GI of 41 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened low-fat milk-based coffee beverages (GI range 35–47, reference food glucose = 100). The dominant glycemic driver is the lactose and added sugar from vanilla syrup and caramel drizzle. The 'less sweet' preparation reduces added sugar by approximately 20%, yielding a GI estimate at the lower end of the range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (41 × 35.9) / 100 ≈ 14.7, rounded to 14.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~52 mg per 100 g; ~190 mg per tall serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of a tall low-fat caramel macchiato is moderate at approximately 190 mg per serving; individuals managing hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium budget of under 2,000 mg and limit frequency of consumption of sweetened coffee beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars from vanilla syrup and caramel drizzle contributing to glycemic load (~14.8 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite the 'less sweet' modification reducing added sugar by approximately 20%, a tall serving still delivers approximately 35 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~14.8; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, consider sugar-free syrup substitutions, and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate allowance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (~0.6 g per 100 g) and use of low-fat milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The use of low-fat milk limits saturated fat to approximately 2.2 g per tall serving, which is within acceptable limits for cardiovascular health; however, the added sugar from syrup and caramel drizzle contributes to overall energy density and should be moderated in individuals with cardiovascular disease risk factors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Low-fat Caramel Macchiato (Less Sweet) is a Starbucks signature espresso-based coffee beverage prepared with steamed low-fat milk, a measured pump of vanilla-flavoured syrup, a double shot of espresso poured over the milk to create the characteristic 'marked' layered appearance, and finished with a caramel sauce drizzle on top. The 'less sweet' customisation reduces the number of vanilla syrup pumps by approximately one pump compared to the standard preparation, lowering the total added sugar content by roughly 20%. The beverage has a creamy, lightly sweet, and mildly bitter flavour profile with a smooth milk-forward texture and a distinctive caramel aroma. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 62 kcal, 3.2 g protein, 1.0 g fat, and 9.8 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-calorie milk-based coffee drink. The low-fat milk base contributes calcium (~110 mg per 100 g), potassium (~155 mg per 100 g), and vitamin B12 (~0.4 mcg per 100 g), supporting bone and neurological health. The glycemic index is estimated at 41 (low GI), but the glycemic load per tall serving is approximately 14.8 due to the volume of carbohydrate delivered. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor this beverage carefully due to its added sugar content from syrup and caramel drizzle, even in the less-sweet variant. Sodium is moderate at ~52 mg per 100 g (~190 mg per serving), relevant for hypertension management. Saturated fat is low at ~0.6 g per 100 g, making it relatively heart-friendly compared to full-fat milk variants. This beverage is widely available at Starbucks outlets across Singapore, including the One Fullerton location, and is a popular choice among coffee drinkers seeking a lighter, lower-calorie version of the classic caramel macchiato.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Starbucks low-fat caramel macchiato?
Is caramel macchiato less sweet healthier?
Low fat caramel macchiato nutrition facts
Starbucks caramel macchiato GI glycemic index
Is caramel macchiato suitable for diabetics?
Low fat coffee drink Singapore calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Starbucks caramel macchiato with low-fat milk, normalised to per-100g basis. The 'less sweet' modifier introduces an estimation step (approximately 20% sugar reduction) that is not directly measured in the source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened low-fat milk-based coffee beverages. No exact HPB Singapore or direct Starbucks Singapore nutrition panel data was available for this specific less-sweet variant. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. The 'less sweet' customisation at Starbucks Singapore typically reduces vanilla syrup by one pump; sugar_g has been adjusted downward by approximately 20% from the standard preparation. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product category; low-fat milk-based beverages contain negligible industrial trans fats. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a liquid milk-based coffee beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee and milk beverages do not contribute meaningful vitamin C. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 6. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 12. GI of 41 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product variant was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 13. Halal status assumed based on Starbucks Singapore standard halal certification; kosher status not confirmed. 14. Serving size of 366 g corresponds to a tall (12 fl oz / 355 ml) cup using a density approximation of 1.03 g/ml.

improvement_actions_taken: Added mandatory Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names for Singapore-served beverage.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported justifications.
Documented all 0.0 values (trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg) with confirmed-zero justifications in notes.
Set glycemic_index to 41 (numeric > 0) with analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values: exact, closest_parent, none.
Ensured natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context.
Set food_group to Beverages (more accurate than input Mixed for a coffee drink).
Included human_condition_name: Type 2 Diabetes in health_context.
